properties.title: Café 't Mandje
properties.description: Café 't Mandje is a historic 'brown café' (traditional Dutch pub) that was opened in 1927 by the openly lesbian Bet van Beeren. It became a safe meeting place for gay men, lesbians, and other colorful visitors at a time when that was highly unusual.

properties.title: Vereniging De Trut
properties.description: Vereniging De Trut is an iconic, non-commercial queer association and club in Amsterdam, founded in 1985 and run entirely by volunteers. Every Sunday, the association hosts the well-known Trut party, an accessible and inclusive nightlife event for the LGBTQIA+ community. Phones must be switched off upon entry. The proceeds from the parties are used through the Trutfonds (Trut Fund) to support LGBTQIA+ initiatives and social projects.

properties.title: Club Church
properties.description: Club Church is a queer nightclub known for its inclusive, sex-positive atmosphere and diverse themed nights. The club, located on Kerkstraat, is open since 2008.

properties.title: Club Raum
properties.description: Club RAUM (since 2024) is a queer club focused on innovative, experimental programming, featuring long nights and weekend events with diverse lineups. Parties often take place across multiple rooms, each with its own atmosphere. Thanks to the strict no-photo and no-video policy, the focus remains on the dance floor and on connecting with one another.

properties.title: Pamela
properties.description: Bar Pamela (opened in 2020) is a colorful queer bar in Amsterdam Oud-West, popular especially with a FLINTA crowd. The bar serves cocktails and vegan-friendly bites in a creative, neon-inspired setting filled with art and music. In the evenings, Pamela often transforms into a lively nightlife venue featuring DJ sets, drag performances, and queer events.

properties.title: Gays and Gadgets
properties.description: Gays & Gadgets is located on Amsterdam’s Spuistraat. With its carefully curated selection of gifts and gadgets, the shop appeals to both gay and straight customers. In addition to tourists, its clientele consists largely of local Amsterdam residents.

properties.title: Park de Oeverlanden
properties.description: De Oeverlanden is a park on the edge of the Amsterdamse Bos that has long been known as a popular meeting place for men who meet other men. Thanks to its sheltered paths and green surroundings, the area offers space for both recreation and informal social encounters.

properties.title: Het Marineterrein
properties.description: The Marineterrein is also known as “Amsterdam’s gay beach,” because many queer men like to gather here in the summer to swim and sunbathe together.

properties.title: RITA Community Center
properties.description: RITA is an Amsterdam-based platform that collects reports of discrimination and unsafe experiences, especially affecting LGBTQ+ people. It supports those affected, provides information, and uses collected data to advocate for better policies and a safer society. You can also walk into their centre in the Reguliersdwarsstraat; it is a meeting space.

properties.title: Rozentuin Vondelpark
properties.description: The Rosarium in Amsterdam's Vondelpark has been a well-known gay cruising spot since the 19th century. Hidden among the rose garden's bushes, generations of 'Rosario's sought romance, intimacy, and connection away from public view.

properties.title: Pink Nieuw West
properties.description: Pink Nieuw-West is a community organization dedicated to strengthening the visibility, safety, and inclusion of LGBTQIA+ people in Amsterdam Nieuw-West. It brings together residents, local organizations, and businesses through events, cultural activities, and community initiatives.

properties.title: Pink Point
properties.description: Pink Point Amsterdam is the city's official LGBTQIA+ information kiosk, located near the Homomonument in the heart of Amsterdam. It provides visitors and locals with information about LGBTQIA+ history, events, nightlife, culture, and community organizations. Pink Point also offers maps, souvenirs, and practical advice, serving as a welcoming starting point for exploring Amsterdam's queer community.

properties.title: Bar Buka
properties.description: Bar Buka is one of Amsterdam’s few dedicated lesbian and queer bars, located in De Pijp, offering a safe and empowering space for LGBTQ+ people and allies. With karaoke nights, DJ events, themed parties, and Pride celebrations, the bar brings people together to celebrate queer culture and connection. Bar Buka creates a warm, inclusive atmosphere where everyone can feel welcome.

properties.title: Boogieland Queerboxing
properties.description: Boogieland Queer Boxing is a beginner-friendly boxing initiative in Amsterdam that creates a safe and inclusive space for LGBTQIA+ people to discover the sport. The Queer Boxing for rookings sessions, every sunday afternoon, focus on learning boxing techniques, building confidence, and connecting with others in a supportive environment without pressure or macho culture.

properties.title: Bar Prik
properties.description: Since 2006, Bar Prik has been an iconic, award-winning queer nightlife venue in the heart of Amsterdam. It offers a friendly, no-nonsense environment where people can relax, enjoy themselves, and feel welcome, both indoors and on its lovely terrace.

properties.title: Lellebel
properties.description: Lellebel, founded in 1997, is known as a welcoming safe space for the LGBTQIA+ community and allies. The trans/queer-owned venue celebrates diversity through drag shows, karaoke nights, comedy, live performances, and other community events.

properties.title: Mezrab
properties.description: Mezrab, the House of Stories, is an Amsterdam-based platform for storytelling and a leading force in the development of storytelling as an art form in the Netherlands. It hosts LGBTQ+ pub quizzes and queer storytelling nights that celebrate diverse voices and experiences. Its events are open to everyone,

properties.title: We Are Queer - Amsterdam Oost
properties.description: A queer gym in Amsterdam East and Amsterdam West, dedicated to creating a safe and inclusive space for queer people to exercise.

properties.title: We Are Queer - Amsterdam West
properties.description: A queer gym in Amsterdam East and Amsterdam West, dedicated to creating a safe and inclusive space for queer people to exercise. The Gym in Amsterdam West is located at Lola Lijn community center.

properties.title: Vrankrijk
properties.description: This political café in the center of Amsterdam combines activism, culture, and community through benefit events and gatherings. It hosts a diverse program including queer nights, punk shows, hip-hop events, vegan dinners, film screenings, benefit concerts, and information evenings,  the café provides a space for solidarity, creativity, and collective action.

properties.title: Tillatec
properties.description: TILLATEC is a nightclub and event space located in a former technical school, combining historic character with a raw, underground atmosphere. The versatile venue hosts everything from 24-hour raves and parties to intimate community gatherings, dinners, and creative events. With unique spaces including former classrooms, courtyards, a garden, and a fully equipped kitchen, TILLATEC offers a distinctive setting for diverse experiences in Amsterdam.

properties.title: ReRoll Works LGBTQIA+ Board Gayming Salon
properties.description: ReRoll Works is a queer-led Amsterdam board game publisher, community hub, and retailer creating inclusive tabletop experiences for LGBTQIA+ players and allies. The store combines playful, bold adult-themed games with community events that bring people together through gaming, humor, and connection.

properties.title: Queer Market Store
properties.description: Queer Market is a platform and permanent store that supports LGBTQIA+ makers, artists, and small businesses by creating space for their work and stories. It offers a welcoming environment where visitors can discover unique art, fashion, crafts, and products while connecting with the queer creative community.

properties.title: IHLIA LGBTI Heritage
properties.description: IHLIA LGBT Heritage is the leading heritage organization for LGBTQIA+ history in the Netherlands and holds the largest LGBTQIA+ collection in Europe. Through its extensive archive, IHLIA preserves the stories, knowledge, and experiences of the past and present, bringing LGBTQIA+ history to life for future generations. It is located on the 3rd floor of the OBA, Amsterdam's public library.

properties.title: Club NYX
properties.description: Club NYX is an inclusive nightclub in the heart of Amsterdam, offering a colorful and playful nightlife experience in a unique four-story former carriage house. Known for its diverse crowd, vibrant atmosphere, and element of surprise, NYX welcomes everyone to dance, celebrate, and express themselves freely.
